text:
Game (, Shōbu) is the 309 th chapter of the Hunter × Hunter manga, written and illustrated by Yoshihiro Togashi.

Synopsis

Shaiapouf suggests the King entertain himself more, and at the latter's request for an explanation, the Royal Guard replies that they should have a competition: as he and Menthuthuyoupi search for Neferpitou, the King will try to catch the two remaining intruders, who are both Nen users. Whoever finds their target first wins. Youpi asks him if he plans to use his scales to hypnotize the rest of the citizens. Pouf replies that he will, so he'll need the King to wait for a while since the difference between his own power and that of the intruders is so great that it would end in a second without being amusing at all; and since they don't know Pitou's location, it may take a while before they find them. So he sets one more condition: the King will use his En only once more. Meruem accepts, promising to grant them one wish each if they win.

The two Royal Guards agree with this. Youpi inquires about the nature of the King's wish, and he and Pouf are appalled to discover that Meruem expects them to reveal what they are hiding from him. Due to their bodies being linked, he can sense their negative emotions; however, because of that, he can also feel their strong loyalty, so he has let them live. If he loses the competition, he will also allow them to keep their secret. Pouf returns to his normal form, swearing he will do his best to spread his scales as fast as possible, without trying to buy time. Meruem repeats the rules for the last time and states that if Pitou returns on his own, he will tell him to hide. He also informs the guards that the remaining Nen users that he perceived through his En before were an octopus and a human carrying something. Pouf reflects that he has to win at all costs, knowing that Pitou and the girl will be a problem. He instructs Youpi not to look for Pitou, but to keep him away from the King. Youpi does not understand the meaning of the handicap he set, considering he cannot split and spread his scales simultaneously, but Pouf tells him to trust him and flies off.

In the meantime, Palm continues to flee after feeling the King's En, still shocked by his aura. She realizes that Meleoron and Knuckle were captured and not killed, so she gazes at Killua with her power. She sees him carrying an unconscious person who, due to the crystal only showing two people, has to be Gon despite his long hair. She wonders what happened while she was not watching and where Pitou is, and silently begs them not to come to the palace. She then spies on Pouf, whom she finds scattering his scales, although she does not understand for what use and why he is far from the King. However, since he is going in the opposite direction, she does not worry about him. Remembering the King's frightening display of power in catching Meleoron and Knuckle, she guesses that she and Ikalgo will be next, and he could very well appear behind her right there and then. She wonders if it would be better to hide underground, where the King's En might not reach, at the cost of risking remaining trapped, or if she should run away from the palace. She is startled to feel a presence near her, but it is only Ikalgo, who, like her, has thought of hiding there and urges her inside. Palm seems on the verge of objecting, but ultimately agrees to go with him. As they run, she tells Ikalgo that Killua is with Gon and that she does not know what happened to Pitou.

Ikalgo drives the truck he has previously stolen and asks Palm why, in her opinion, Meleoron and Knuckle are still alive. Palm says she isn't even sure they are, just hoping so. However, Ikalgo states that he believes they are alive for a hostage exchange. He is determined to set a trap, starting with hiding Komugi in Bizeff's room. Once they arrive, he tells Palm to gather the four girls that worked under the secretary and suggests she tie their hands and blindfold them to avoid panic. She agrees and inquires as to why he has taken Welfin and an unconscious Bloster along.

The reason is revealed shortly afterward when Ikalgo asks Welfin to be his messenger: he will tell the King or the Royal Guards that they are in the underground vault and they have the girl. To avoid casting suspicion on himself, Welfin makes clear that he will have to inform them that it is a trap, to which Ikalgo agrees. Still, Welfin considers not going along with it in his hand, thinking about the danger he risks in relaying such a message to the King. But he tells Ikalgo he'll do it, and takes the truck. Palm, due to his key role in their strategy, wants to watch him with her power even if it means being unable to observe Pouf any longer, but Ikalgo stops her. He then tells Welfin that he will be free after he delivers the message, and suggests he go and see Gyro. Welfin gets mad at him, but Ikalgo calls him by his true name, Zaiqahal, which Welfin had never disclosed to anyone. Ikalgo reveals that, although he does not remember his own name, in his former life, he was his comrade. He then asks Welfin if he considers the Chimera Ants the enemy of their NGL. The wolf curses himself for giving in as he drives the truck out of the hangar, while Meruem is bothered by something, with the word "game" lingering in his mind.

Characters in Order of Appearance

Trivia

Although Viz translated them differently, the chapter has the same title as Chapter 206.
